pr.title: Fix `check-issues` error output and existing code issues
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Fixed CI check-issues behavior and cleaned up existing code issues in sysdolphin baselib headers/source. The workflow no longer captures docker output into a shell variable or writes it into GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY, instead letting the check container emit directly. Code fixes addressed C prototype hygiene with explicit void parameter lists, added missing typedef names for structs, guarded matching-only static data behind MUST_MATCH, and made an explicit but documented cast for GXSetTevOp.
what_changed: Changed the build workflow's 'Check for code issues' step to run the docker container directly rather than storing its output in result, echoing it, and appending it to GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.
In HSD_SetupTevStage, cast desc->u.tevconf.clr_op to GXTevMode for GXSetTevOp and added a todo noting the cast is incorrect.
Updated static allocator definitions TevAlloc and CnstAlloc from empty parameter lists to explicit void parameter lists.
Wrapped static GXTevRegID id[7] and list_type string data in src/sysdolphin/baselib/texp.c with #ifdef MUST_MATCH.
Added typedef aliases to previously anonymous typedef struct declarations: HSD_TExpRes in texp.h and HSD_TExpDag in texpdag.h.
Updated inline no-argument video accessors HSD_VIGetNbXFB, HSD_VIGetVIStatus, and HSD_VIGetRenderMode to use explicit void parameter lists.
smart_moves: Letting the check-issues docker command write directly to standard output avoids masking or reformatting error output through shell capture and step-summary formatting.
The GXSetTevOp cast resolves a type issue while preserving evidence that the underlying type relationship still needs decomp investigation via an explicit @todo.
Using #ifdef MUST_MATCH for otherwise-unused matching artifacts keeps source available for matching builds without polluting normal builds or triggering code-issue checks.
Adding typedef names to struct declarations makes header APIs consistent with later uses such as HSD_TExpRes* and HSD_TExpDag*.
Replacing old-style empty C parameter lists with (void) removes ambiguity and satisfies stricter code checking without altering behavior.
decomp_lessons: When decomp-only or matching-only static data is required for layout or symbol matching but unused in normal builds, guard it with MUST_MATCH rather than deleting it.
Do not silently paper over type mismatches in graphics API calls; if a cast is needed for the current state, document uncertainty with a todo so future work can revisit the struct or enum typing.
Headers should provide complete typedef names when downstream declarations use the typedef form, especially for shared baselib structs.
Use explicit void in no-argument C function declarations and definitions to satisfy modern checks and avoid old-style C prototype semantics.
CI issue-checking scripts should preserve raw diagnostic output where possible; wrapping output into summaries can hide formatting or failure behavior.
naming_conventions: Baselib struct typedefs use HSD_* names matching the struct tag, as in struct HSD_TExpRes typedefed to HSD_TExpRes and struct HSD_TExpDag typedefed to HSD_TExpDag.
No-argument HSD accessors retain HSD_VIGet* naming but should be declared as function(void), for example HSD_VIGetNbXFB(void).
Matching-specific build guards use the existing MUST_MATCH macro.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved matching-related static objects id[7] and list_type only under MUST_MATCH, indicating they are relevant to matching builds but problematic for regular code-issue checks.
The PR did not include assembly diff evidence beyond the MUST_MATCH guard and no explicit reviewer discussion about matching strategy.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs resolving the @todo Incorrect cast in HSD_SetupTevStage or changing the type of desc->u.tevconf.clr_op.
Search for additional no-argument functions declared with empty parameter lists that may fail check-issues.
Search for matching-only static data that should be guarded by MUST_MATCH.
Review check-issues workflow changes after PR 1047 to see whether direct docker output remained the preferred pattern.
